For project-based businesses, keeping track of aging work in progress (WIP) is essential for maintaining healthy cash flow. But identifying overdue WIP, understanding its root causes, and deciding what action to take often requires time-consuming analysis across multiple reports.

AI-assisted aging work in progress analytics and management helps project financial controllers gain these insights faster. Using Joule, users can analyze and visualize WIP aging across defined time buckets, while AI automatically generates executive summaries that highlight critical items and recommend the next steps. Intelligent drill-down navigation and automated billing notifications make it easier to investigate issues and accelerate resolution.

By transforming complex project data into actionable insights, this innovation helps finance and project teams reduce manual analysis, speed up billing cycles, lower cash flow risks, and spend more time making informed decisions instead of creating reports.

 

 

Summary 

🎦Register for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series! 

Between July 13-17, we are hosting a series of 24 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. On our landing page you can register for the entire series or pick the sessions most relevant to you. 

 

🔎Find all our assets  

The release 2608 showcases more than 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Implementing IFRS S1 and S2 Sustainability Disclosure Standards with End‑to‑End Reporting Flows
entry.content:
This article reflects the collective efforts of several contributors: Bilge Akdur, Elsa Sandoval Ruiz, Julia Sabrowski

Introduction

The IFRS® Sustainability Disclosure Standards, IFRS S1 (General Requirements for Disclosure of Sustainability-Related Financial Information) and IFRS S2 (Climate-Related Disclosures), issued in June 2023 and effective for annual reporting periods beginning on or after 1 January 2024, are a common baseline for sustainability-related financial disclosure. As a growing number of jurisdictions around the world are adopting these standards, organizations are increasingly expected to report on climate-related risks and opportunities comparable to those applied in financial reporting. Sustainability reporting is dynamic by design as it considers material impacts, risks, and opportunities. Investors, for example, can now look to sustainability KPIs as signals to move from mostly stewardship non-financial reporting to financial-grade sustainability analysis for sustainability strategic business decisions.

For organizations reporting under the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) or implementing IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards, reporting requirements are increasingly interconnected across frameworks. Consequently, interoperability is considered a critical enabler for organizations to align sustainability data across multiple regulatory and voluntary standards. SAP Sustainability solutions adopt interoperability as a characteristic for companies reporting under multiple frameworks, which can increasingly consolidate their sustainability data management rather than duplicating effort across disconnected processes.

End-to-end sustainability reporting requires an auditable chain from operational cause through identification, collection, calculation, analysis, and disclosure. Point tools own a slice of that chain, while SAP owns the whole of it. SAP Sustainability Control Tower and SAP Sustainability Footprint Management together enable an end‑to‑end IFRS S1 and S2 sustainability reporting flow, including carbon footprint calculation, and climate- and sustainability-related data acquisition through to governed disclosures. SAP Sustainability Control Tower now provides SAP‑delivered IFRS S1 and S2 data points as master content, so organizations can manage IFRS S1 and S2 disclosures within the same governance framework they already use for ESRS reporting, while SAP Sustainability Footprint Management supplies the underlying emissions and activity data needed for IFRS S2 climate‑related metrics. This post walks through the integrated flow from initial setup and data integration through to AI‑assisted report creation.

Initial Setup and Master Data

The starting point for IFRS S1 and S2 reporting in SAP Sustainability Control Tower is the Manage Master Data application. Here, organizations configure the foundational structures that underpin their sustainability disclosures, including reporting structures such as legal entities, business locations, and organizational units, as well as the associated hierarchies that define the reporting boundary.

Now, the reporting requirements under IFRS S1 and S2, version 2023, are provided by SAP in the “Reporting Standard”, “Reporting Standard Version,” and “Data Points” dimensions in the Manage Master Data App as of July 1st, 2026.

For IFRS reporting specifically, the same organizational reporting structure used to support ESRS disclosures is reused. This means organizations already configured for CSRD reporting do not need to rebuild their hierarchy levels, fiscal year variants, and organizational mappings, providing a unified data foundation across both ESRS and IFRS disclosure requirements.

Metrics Coverage

In the Manage Metrics application, organizations can now access SAP-provided metrics mapped to IFRS S1 and S2 data points.

As of July 2026, SAP Sustainability Control Tower includes:

  • 24 quantitative metrics for IFRS S2 Climate-Related Disclosures, covering greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1, 2, and 3) and carbon credits..
  • 57 qualitative metrics across IFRS S1 and S2, including:
    • IFRS S1 General Disclosures: 13 metrics
    • IFRS S1 and IFRS S2 shared General Disclosures: 15 metrics
    • IFRS S2 Climate-Related Disclosures: 29 metrics

To support interoperability, qualitative metrics that appear in both IFRS S1 and IFRS S2 are represented as a single metric where applicable. This avoids duplicating data entry for disclosures that serve both standards.

Data points that include explanatory content, implementation guidance, or reporting clarifications are out of scope and are not included in the delivered master data. Organizations can refer to the corresponding IFRS standards documentation for this information.

Within metric disclosure references, customers can see, when applicable, interoperability between disclosure requirements across standards such as ESRS and IFRS, helping organizations address multiple reporting frameworks through a single reporting effort.

Reporting Scope

Once metrics are available, organizations define their reporting scope: which material metrics apply to which parts of their reporting structure, and for which periods. The reporting scope configuration in SAP Sustainability Control Tower allows organizations to select IFRS-relevant metrics alongside their existing ESRS scope.

This is particularly relevant for organizations reporting under both ESRS and IFRS, where many climate-related disclosures overlap. Because SAP Sustainability Control Tower supports interoperability with standards in its metric definitions, organizations can configure their reporting scope once and satisfy data requirements for multiple frameworks without managing parallel reporting processes.

Data Acquisition

With the reporting scope defined, data acquisition follows established SAP Sustainability Control Tower patterns. Quantitative data for IFRS S2 climate metrics can be sourced through the same channels used for ESRS climate data:

  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management integration for greenhouse gas emissions data across scope 1, 2 and 3, with configurable period granularity (monthly, quarterly, yearly) based on the active fiscal year variant.
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management energy and fuel mix metrics for IFRS‑relevant climate information, adding energy consumption, energy mix, fuel mix, energy content and sourcing type, calculated together with the emissions in a single run.
  • SAP Datasphere content packages for loading actuals from connected source systems.
  • File upload for data not available through system integration.
  • Inbound ODATA API for programmatic data ingestion from any source.

For qualitative metrics, the data collection process supports form-based collection with workflow governance. Organizations can group IFRS qualitative metrics within a single data collection form, assign processors and approvers, and manage the entire collection cycle through governed workflows. It also supports collection of the corporate summary while showing previously recorded data for comparison.

Emissions and Energy Data

For IFRS S2 greenhouse gas disclosures, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management calculates corporate emissions directly based on ERP master and transactional data, rather than from manual uploads or separate data silos. This creates one governed data foundation for Scope 1, 2 and 3 results that aligns climate metrics with the same operational events and structures used to run the business.

In the same calculation flow, the solution can also deliver energy‑related metrics such as energy consumption, energy mix, fuel mix, energy content and sourcing type, where these are relevant for IFRS S2 reporting. One run of the model, therefore, produces multiple climate‑related outputs: mandatory emissions data plus optional energy metrics to support transition analysis and target tracking.

Because activity data, emission factors and calculation logic are all traceable back to their source, the resulting IFRS S2 figures are easier to substantiate. When transferred into SAP Sustainability Control Tower via direct integration, this governance and lineage are preserved, connecting operational cause, calculation and disclosure in a single, auditable chain - eliminating the need for external transformation or aggregation steps.

Note: SAP Sustainability Control Tower's emissions-related Data Provider Interfaces (including SAP_SCT_DPI_Emission and SAP_SCT_DPI_Energy) provide the structure for ingesting this data from SAP and non-SAP sources.

Analytics

Analyze ESG Data application enables organizations to explore their IFRS-aligned sustainability performance. With the target-versus-actual visualization capabilities delivered in Q2 2026, organizations can compare their climate-related performance against defined targets directly within the application.

For IFRS S2, this is particularly relevant for transition plan monitoring: organizations can track progress toward their greenhouse gas reduction targets, visualize emissions trends across reporting periods, and drill down by reporting structure to identify areas requiring attention.

The flexible chart-specific configuration model allows independent control over metric selection, filters, drilldowns, and chart types. Users can display up to six charts in parallel and combine chart and table views for side-by-side analysis of different IFRS disclosure dimensions, such as comparing Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions performance across legal entities or time periods.

 

AI-Assisted Report Generation

Generate Sustainability Reports with AI is available free of charge to all SAP Sustainability Control Tower customers as a base AI feature. Today, customers can already use the Generic ESG Reporting Template to automatically draft ESG reports in multiple languages, preview and edit the content, and download the result in PDF or DOCX format, with dynamic AI‑driven revisions helping refine the final narrative.

Building on these capabilities, the Generate ESG Reports with AI application will include an IFRS‑specific report template as a roadmap item for H2 2026. This template will allow organizations to generate a structured IFRS sustainability disclosure report from the quantitative and qualitative data managed in SAP Sustainability Control Tower, drawing on the governed ESG data foundation to produce a draft aligned with IFRS S1 and S2 requirements. Using the IFRS template, AI‑assisted report generation will cover the core IFRS content pillars—Governance, Strategy, Risk Management, and Metrics and Targets—providing a standards‑aligned starting point that customers can further tailor to their specific context.

 Interoperability: ESRS and IFRS Side by Side

A key design principle behind the IFRS content in SAP Sustainability Control Tower is interoperability. Organizations reporting under both ESRS (for CSRD compliance) and IFRS (for jurisdictions adopting ISSB standards) can manage both from a single data foundation.

Where disclosure requirements overlap, particularly in general disclosures and climate-related reporting, SAP Sustainability Control Tower's metric definitions are designed so that data entered once can satisfy requirements across both frameworks. Within the metric general information, organizations can see the mapping between ESRS and IFRS disclosure requirements, reducing the effort required to maintain parallel reporting tracks.

Additional Information

Documentation on IFRS S1 and S2 metrics is available in SAP Help Portal for SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Scenario Guidance for Corporate Carbon Calculation is available in SAP Help Portal for SAP Sustainability Footprint Management.

This article contains copyright material of the IFRS Foundation and SAP All rights reserved. Published by SAP under license from the IFRS Foundation. Reproduction and use rights are strictly limited.  For more information about the IFRS Foundation and rights to use its material please visit www.ifrs.org. © 2026 IFRS Foundation

entry.title: Q2 2026 Innovations: SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
Q2 went by fast! I hope that you all were able to participate in SAPPHIRE 2026 either in person or virtually. The SAP Autonomous Enterprise was announced and a shout out of huge thanks to our service customers Pfizer and Doehler for sharing your incredible stories & insights about your transformation, AI adoption and value outcomes with SAP Service Cloud and SAP ESM! Our next value-add event is queued up -  SAP Connect 2026 is coming to Las Vegas, October 5-7 and we welcome our customers and partners to secure an early bird rate by August 10th to discover how to maximize value of your solutions, explore best practices in AI and business transformation approaches and much, much more!

Before I jump into our Q2 innovations for SAP ESM there are a couple of exciting opportunities I’d like to share.

  • It’s not too late to join our SAP ESM webcast on Tuesday, July 21st, focused on HR Service Delivery: AI, Data, and Experience: From Transactions to Intelligent HR Services also featuring a widely recognized expert Anita Lettink. If you’re reading post-event, click the link above and watch the replay!
  • Also, there is a new SAP Learning module for SAP ESM: Integrating SAP Enterprise Service Management. This course is available at no cost and is designed specifically for administrators, consultants, and business users to deepen their knowledge of SAP Enterprise Service Management. This course focuses on integration configuration to enable execution of end-to-end scenarios delivering true value and operational efficiency across the organization.

Now back to the focus of this blog – our Q2 innovations for SAP ESM! There are so many innovations and improvements please register for our Q2 Innovation webcast July 29th hosted by our product team.

Self-Service Catalog

The Self-Service Catalog provides employees with a guided, intuitive way to find and request the services they need, without requiring an understanding of the underlying processes or systems. Requests submitted through the appropriate topic catalog or tile are automatically resolved or routed based on the inquiry context, which reduces manual triage effort, frustration in solutioning and shortens resolution times. For service agents, this means fewer incomplete or misrouted cases and more time spent on higher-value work. For employees, it means a simpler, more predictable experience when engaging with their enterprise or shared service teams.

Service Catalog configuration

                                                                                             

Service Catalog Self Service

 

Content Package Framework

The Content Package Framework enables organizations to deploy SAP ESM more quickly by leveraging prebuilt, reusable configurations for case types, workflows, and catalogs. Rather than building service processes from the ground up, customers and SI partners can start from proven templates and adapt them to specific business needs. This reduces implementation effort, simplifies rollout across lines of business, and helps organizations standardize service delivery at scale. Content packages for HR service delivery are planned for a future release.

New Email Editor

The new email editor provides service agents with a modern, streamlined interface for composing, editing, and managing customer communications. Designed with an AI-first architecture in mind, the editor supports faster email creation while maintaining the quality and consistency expected in service interactions. Coupled with the AI email drafter capability, agents truly spend less time formatting and more time focused on resolving customer issues, which improves response times and communication quality.

 

Email editor

Creation of Sales Objects from the Customer Hub

While this capability is more closely aligned with SAP Service Cloud, sales support and B2B supplier support are common use cases we see deployed for SAP ESM as well. With that said, service agents can now create and manage leads, opportunities, appointments, and sales orders directly from the service agent desktop when the interaction calls for it, reducing clicks and upskilling time. The capability supports closer alignment between service and sales teams and delivers that seamless experience you expect between SAP solutions.

 Self-Service Enhancements

Two enhancements provide administrators with greater control over the self-service experience:

  • Edit Form in Self-Service During Case Lifecycle feature allows administrators to define conditions under which a form remains editable, giving employees the ability to update information at appropriate points in the case lifecycle without agent involvement.
  • Adapt Case Details Screen per Case Type feature allows administrators to configure the Case Details screen in Self-Service on a per-case-type basis, ensuring that users only see fields and actions that are relevant to their specific scenario.

Together, these enhancements help drive higher self-service adoption and reduce unnecessary agent handoffs.

Embedded Component for Custom Services

Administrators can now define list views or object forms within a custom service and embed them into custom user interfaces, as well as selected standard UIs. By mapping parameters from the host page, related custom records can be viewed and worked on directly within the relevant UI view. This capability makes it easier to extend SAP ESM to fit unique business processes and ensures that agents and users have access to contextual information exactly where they need it, without navigating away from their current task.

New Self-Service Experience (Restricted Release)

A new Self-Service experience for SAP ESM is being introduced as a restricted release. Aligned with the latest self-service catalog capabilities, the new experience is designed to support cross-functional deployment across the enterprise, providing instant solutions to common inquiries and enabling contextual case creation and management. As this is a restricted release, customers interested in exploring the capability are encouraged to request access via the solution. Early feedback from customers will play an important role in shaping the general availability release, and participation is strongly encouraged.

Sign In as Another User

Administrators can now sign in as another business user seeing the same functionality and authorizations as that user. This allows administrators to reproduce and troubleshoot issues from the user’s perspective without screen sharing or password exchange. This results in faster issue resolution, stronger security practices, and a more efficient support process for both administrators and end users.

Analytics Improvements

This release delivers two important improvements to analytics capabilities:

  • Embedded SAC Widgets allow SAP Analytics Cloud visualizations to be embedded directly into the SAP ESM interface, displaying focused analytics with individual tiles within the daily flow of various business objects such as cases, customers and opportunities. These widgets can also be incorporated into mashups for more comprehensive analytics overall.
  • Custom Service Entities in Analytics allow custom service entities to be used as data sources in SAP Analytics Cloud. Once a custom entity is marked as Analytics Relevant, the system automatically generates a corresponding data source that appears under Custom Data Sources in the Query Designer, where it can be combined with standard data sources to produce tailored analytics content.

These enhancements bring insights closer to where work happens and reduce the need to switch between systems for data-driven decision-making.

Approval Process Simplification

Two enhancements to approval processes help organizations improve consistency and reduce delays.

  • Configure Approval and Rejection Reasons: Administrators can now configure predefined approval and rejection reasons, create custom reason lists, assign them to specific approval processes, and control whether reason selection is mandatory or optional. This supports more consistent decision-making and improves auditability across approval workflows.
  • Automatic Approval Settings: Configuration allowing approval steps to be automatically approved or rejected if no action is taken within a defined duration can be defined from one hour to ten days. This helps ensure that workflows continue to progress even when approvers are unavailable, reducing bottlenecks and shortening overall case cycle times.

Accelerating Case Resolution with UX Simplicity and Automation

Several enhancements in this release focus on simplifying the case experience and giving administrators more configuration control. A few examples include:

  • Case Data Retention Rules by Case Type: This feature allows administrators to configure retention rules for automated transaction depersonalization on a per-case-type basis, helping the organization meet legal, regional, and business-specific data retention requirements.
  • Multiple Catalogs Mapped to One Case Type: The ability to map multiple catalogs to one case type allows administrators to assign additional category catalogs to a case type, minimizing configuration effort where similar case types can be used across various business functions.
  • Hide Category Levels in Case: This feature allows administrators to selectively hide specific category levels within cases, providing agents with a cleaner and more focused interface by removing unnecessary hierarchical information.

Case adaptation

These enhancements help streamline the agent experience while giving administrators the flexibility to align case management with compliance, business, and operational requirements.

Useful Information

For additional Q2 innovations relevant for SAP Enterprise Service Management please reference Kirsi Tarvainen’s blog on SAP Service Cloud and register for the upcoming Q2 Release Webinar. To stay up-to-date as we release capabilities each month and to get a full view of what’s new for Q2, reference the What’s New page in our help portal. Also, reference these valuable links for more on SAP ESM and SAP CX solutions:

SAP CX Q2 2020 Innovation News

SAP Learning ESM Course

SAP Service Cloud V2 Certification Course

SAP Release Navigator

SAP ESM’s free trial Get hands-on experience today with an HR service delivery scenario (Finance shared services coming soon).

Use the SAP Enterprise Service Management managed tag when posting on SAP ESM topics in the community!

And finally, thank you. The continued growth of this community, and the willingness of its members to share, question, and collaborate, is what moves SAP ESM forward. Every SAP employee, partner, and customer benefits from the collective knowledge exchanged here and it’s genuinely appreciated.

entry.title: What’s new in Artificial Intelligence in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2608
entry.content:
AI is rapidly evolving from a productivity enhancer into a true business collaborator. With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release, SAP takes another significant step toward its vision of the Autonomous Enterprise, where AI is no longer a separate tool but an integral part of everyday business processes. New innovations across agentic AI, Joule, and embedded AI capabilities help organizations automate routine work, uncover insights faster, and streamline decision-making—while keeping people firmly in control.

In this blog, we'll explore the most important AI innovations delivered with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. These enhancements are relevant for a wide range of roles, empowering teams in finance, supply chain, professional services, and beyond to make faster, more informed decisions and focus on strategic business outcomes.

Joule Work

In this release, we are thrilled to introduce Joule Work, a dynamic workspace that fundamentally reimagines how work gets done across your enterprise. Going far beyond a simple chat, Joule Work acts as your central engagement layer, intelligently interpreting your intent to surface the right insights, automate routine work, and coordinate powerful AI agents across all your systems. By unifying conversations and AI-generated spaces for complex problem-solving, it transforms siloed tasks into a single, seamless experience. This allows your teams to move away from transactional processes and focus on what truly matters: making the critical decisions that move the business forward.

Conversations

Conversations in Joule Work are designed to facilitate natural language interactions, enabling users to quickly ask questions and receive insights directly within the workspace. This functionality makes accessing data and obtaining immediate answers effortless, breaking down communication barriers and accelerating decision-making processes. Conversations integrate seamlessly with your existing systems, allowing for real-time interactions, making sure users have the information they need at their fingertips without jumping between multiple applications.

Spaces

Spaces are dynamic, AI-generated environments within Joule Work that adapt to facilitate complex problem-solving and collaborative tasks. Unlike static dashboards or document sharing platforms, Spaces intelligently structure resources and tools according to the needs of the task at hand. Spaces provide an interactive canvas where information, analytics, and dialogue converge. This flexibility supports diverse business processes, encouraging innovation, efficiency, and teamwork across departments.

Develop

The Develop capability, facilitated through Joule Studio, empowers organizations to create and deploy custom agents and automations tailored to their specific business processes. This feature puts the power of AI in your hands, allowing both developers and business users to design specialized workflows that seamlessly integrate with existing systems. Develop enables businesses to extend the functionality of Joule Work beyond out-of-the-box solutions, accommodating unique requirements and enhancing scalability. With intuitive tools and support for advanced scripting, users can craft intelligent automations that optimize operations and drive productivity.

 

SAP-managed Joule

The SAP-managed approach for Joule is our new, recommended method for activating and operating your generative AI copilot. It's designed to dramatically simplify your experience and accelerate time-to-value by replacing the complex, manual setup with a streamlined, self-service activation process directly within the SAP for Me portal. With SAP handling the technical management and integration, your team is freed from significant IT overhead. This new model also ensures you are best positioned to receive the latest Joule innovations, including new agents and capabilities like Joule Work, as soon as they become available, often with minimal to no additional configuration required on your part.

 

Autonomous Finance

Financial Close Assistant (release 2608.03)

Financial close demands tight coordination across clearing, reconciliation, journal entries, and analysis—all of this under deadline pressure. The Financial Closing Assistant empowers the Financial Close Accountant to orchestrate an intelligent agent-based team that handles these tasks autonomously within set boundaries. From receivables clearing to intercompany reconciliation and key performance indicator analysis, agents execute critical close activities with transparency and audit-ready precision, enabling a faster, more reliable close.

 

Receivables and Payables Clearing Agent (release 2608.02)

Finance teams can now streamline accounts receivable and payable clearing with the new Receivables and Payables Clearing Agent, an AI-powered capability that analyzes open items and generates balanced clearing proposals for accountant review. Integrated with dedicated SAP Fiori apps, the agent intelligently matches invoices and open items using commonly used reference fields and provides clear explanations for its recommendations.

By accelerating clearing activities, reducing manual matching effort, and minimizing human error, the solution helps improve operational efficiency while giving accountants greater confidence in the review process. Approved proposals can be automatically posted as clearing documents, enabling a faster and more streamlined financial close process.

 

Journal Entry Agent (release 2608.03)

Creating journal entries from spreadsheet data is now faster and more efficient with the Journal Entry Agent. This AI-powered capability analyzes uploaded spreadsheet files submitted through Joule and automatically generates accurate, compliant journal entry proposals. Users can review, adjust, and post these proposals directly in the Manage Journal Entry Proposals app, significantly reducing manual effort in journal entry processing.

By automatically mapping spreadsheet columns to the required journal entry fields, the agent helps improve accuracy, increase transparency through access to source files, and streamline financial workflows. Users are notified when proposals are ready for review and can manage the complete lifecycle—from review and adjustment to posting—within a single application. The result is a more efficient journal entry process that supports faster period-end closing while maintaining transparency and control.

 

International Trade Classification Agent

Classifying products for international trade can be a complex and time-consuming process, requiring deep knowledge of customs regulations and commodity code structures. The International Trade Classification Agent helps simplify this task by using AI to analyze product characteristics and automatically recommend appropriate commodity codes and customs tariff numbers for international shipping.

Designed to support trade classification specialists, the agent provides transparent, explainable recommendations based on product information and applicable trade regulations. Each proposed classification is accompanied by a clear rationale, making it easier for users to review, validate, and approve results. By automating classification activities and reducing reliance on manual research, the solution helps organizations save time, improve compliance, and minimize the risk of errors through a robust and auditable decision-making process.

 

E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent (release 2608.02)

As electronic invoicing mandates continue to expand globally, organizations face increasing pressure to resolve invoice submission errors quickly to avoid disruptions in order-to-cash processes. The E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent helps simplify and accelerate error handling by providing contextual insights, recommended resolution actions, and guided root cause analysis directly within the workflow.

The agent analyzes and classifies technical error messages returned by tax authorities and submission networks, distinguishing between technical issues—such as connectivity, infrastructure, or data-format problems—and business-impacting issues that require changes to document content, master data, or financial postings. By delivering standardized guidance, clear explanations, and documented reasoning for each proposed resolution, the solution promotes consistent error handling, improves auditability, and helps organizations meet mandatory submission deadlines while reducing the risk of non-compliance penalties.

 

Statutory Report Analysis Agent (release 2608.02)

Reviewing statutory and VAT reports can be a time-consuming process, particularly for organizations managing large volumes of tax declarations across multiple reporting periods. The Statutory Report Analysis Agent helps tax and finance teams reduce this effort by automatically analyzing reporting trends, identifying anomalies, and performing root cause analysis to uncover potential compliance risks before reports are submitted.

By comparing current reporting results with historical periods, the agent detects fluctuations, unusual transactions, and outliers that may require attention. It analyzes contributing factors across dimensions such as tax codes, document types, and business partners, and provides consolidated summaries of key figures, identified issues, and root cause findings. In addition, the agent delivers actionable recommendations to help users validate variances, investigate irregularities, and make informed decisions with confidence. The result is a more efficient and data-driven review process that improves compliance readiness without increasing manual effort.

 

Autonomous Supply Chain

Production Master Data Readiness Agent (release 2608.02)

The Product Master Data Readiness Agent assists Master Data Specialists in efficiently creating and updating manufacturing master data, ensuring faster and more accurate preparation for production. The agent enables quick production initiation with minimal manual effort when setting up new materials, prevents production interruptions, and supports consistent planning through the application of proven patterns.

It proactively addresses master data quality issues to avoid unexpected problems during production. For existing product masters with incomplete or potentially incorrect data, the agent suggests values for MRP-related fields using SAP-RPT-1 and similar materials' context.

Additionally, it examines available routings and suggests reusable or copyable options using a machine learning algorithm to identify similar BOMs and their routing through production version tracing

 

Production Planning and Operation agent

The Production Planning and Operations Agent enables users to release production orders through natural language in Joule. It allows for a response to automated checks concerning material, capacity, and scheduling availability by selecting suggested alternative components and adjusting schedules. Users can approve these changes to authorize Joule to release the orders.

 

Inventory Supervisor agent (release 2608.02)

Keeping inventory under control requires continuous visibility into stock performance and the ability to quickly respond to emerging issues. The Inventory Supervisor Agent helps inventory managers turn inventory KPIs into corrective action by providing end-to-end transparency into stock situations, proactively identifying inventory risks, and recommending optimization measures.

The agent continuously monitors inventory performance and performs real-time and ad-hoc stock analysis to detect situations that require attention, such as slow-moving or obsolete inventory. It goes beyond issue detection by analyzing root causes, assessing business impact in terms of quantity and value, and providing actionable recommendations to improve inventory health. By automating analysis and delivering guided insights, the solution reduces manual effort, enables faster decision-making, and helps organizations proactively address inventory challenges before they impact operations.

 

Professional Services

Smart Suggestions for Time Entry (release 2608)

With release 2608, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces Smart Suggestions for Time Entry, an AI-powered capability that automatically proposes timesheet entries based on an employee’s recent booking history and recurring work patterns. Instead of manually searching for projects, tasks, and cost objects, employees can review and confirm pre-populated suggestions with a single click, significantly reducing administrative effort and improving user experience. By streamlining time recording, the feature helps increase on-time submissions, reduce booking errors, and provide more accurate project cost data for project managers and finance teams. As the system learns from individual booking behavior, suggestions become increasingly relevant, enabling faster, more reliable, and effortless time tracking.

 

Environment, Health, and Safety

AI-assisted compliance management (release 2608)

With the 2608 release, SAP S/4HANA Cloud introduces AI-assisted compliance management for Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS), helping organizations accelerate the setup of compliance obligations from regulatory and permit documents. AI automatically extracts and structures relevant information from PDF source documents, identifies compliance requirements, and proposes corresponding tasks such as maintenance activities, notifications, and reporting actions. A side-by-side document viewer provides full traceability between source content and generated obligations, improving audit readiness while reducing manual effort, data-entry errors, and the risk of overlooking compliance-relevant requirements. This innovation enables faster, more accurate, and scalable compliance management across complex regulatory landscapes.

 

Joule for Consultants

Custom Knowledge Grounding

Organizations can now enhance SAP Joule for Consultants with Custom Knowledge Grounding, enabling administrators to connect company-specific documents and knowledge sources through SAP AI Core. By combining organizational content with SAP-curated expertise, Joule delivers more relevant, context-aware, and tailored responses. This helps consultants spend less time searching across multiple sources and make faster, better-informed decisions that align with their organization's standards and best practices.

 

Expert Workspace

The new Expert Workspace provides a powerful way to organize and manage AI Experts—specialized AI assistants configured with custom instructions and optional reference materials for more focused, context-rich interactions. Users can now create up to 25 Experts, supporting a wider range of projects, workflows, and areas of expertise.

To improve transparency and management, Expert Workspace now displays the user's total attachment storage allocation of 1 GB across all Experts, while maintaining a 10 MB per file upload limit. Enhanced search, tagging, and filtering capabilities make it easier to locate and organize Experts, ensuring quick access even as expert libraries grow.

Centralized Expert Library for Organizations

Administrators can now establish a Centralized Expert Library, allowing organizations to curate, manage, and publish reusable Experts for all users. Through a streamlined workflow, organizations can capture expert knowledge from experienced team members and make it available across the business, promoting consistency, knowledge sharing, and faster onboarding. The library remains private to the customer organization, while administrators retain full control over publishing, updating, and retiring shared Experts.

UI and Performance Enhancements

SAP Joule for Consultants continues to evolve with several usability and productivity improvements designed to create a more seamless user experience.

A refreshed welcome screen provides a modernized entry point and easier access to key capabilities. Users can now maintain up to 50 active conversations, a significant increase from the previous limit of 10, enabling more effective management of parallel projects and workstreams.

The introduction of a Retry capability allows users to regenerate responses without restarting the conversation. Whether the goal is to create a longer explanation, a more concise summary, or incorporate additional instructions, answers can be refined with just a few clicks. In addition, users can now edit their original prompts, making it easier to add context, clarify requirements, and improve response quality without beginning a new conversation. Together, these enhancements make interactions with Joule more flexible, efficient, and collaborative.

 

The Future is Intelligent
The 2608 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition demonstrates our commitment to building an ERP that is not just a system of record, but a true partner in your success. By embedding AI across the suite, we are helping you unlock new levels of productivity, enhance decision-making, and create a more intuitive and enjoyable user experience for everyone.

This is just the beginning, and I can't wait to see how you leverage these new capabilities to drive your business forward.

 

🎦Register for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series! 

Between July 13-17, we are hosting a series of 24 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. On our landing page you can register for the entire series or pick the sessions most relevant to you. 

🔎Find all our assets  

The release 2608 showcases more than 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: One Configuration Switch Unlocks Invoice Receipt Date Tracking for Every Country
entry.content:
For years, the Invoice Receipt Date on the accounting document header was a assigned to a handful of country localizations like the Czech VAT Ledger. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608, that changes: one configuration switch now activates the field for every company code in every country/region.

Scope

Scope Note: This blog post covers SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 and later. The feature is delivered through Configuration Activity 107263 — Activate Invoice Receipt Date, application component FI-LOC-FI, and applies to scope item J60 (Accounts Payable). The activation is a customer-managed setting: no SAP Note or manual coding is required.

Why the Invoice Receipt Date Matters

The Invoice Receipt Date — stored in the classic field BKPF-REINDAT — captures the day your organization actually received a supplier invoice. It is deliberately distinct from the three dates you already know:

  • Document Date (BLDAT) — the date printed on the supplier's invoice.
  • Posting Date (BUDAT) — the date that drives the accounting period.
  • Entry Date (CPUDT) — the system timestamp when the document was entered.

Legislation and internal governance increasingly measure payment performance from the moment the invoice arrives at your door — not from the invoice date and not from the posting date. Examples include the EU Late Payment Directive, the UK Payment Practices Reporting obligation for large companies, and dozens of country-specific VAT and audit rules. Without a dedicated field, teams end up storing the receipt date in a custom Z-field, misusing the baseline date, or reconstructing it from workflow logs — none of which reports cleanly.

Activating the standard field solves all three problems at once: it becomes visible in every relevant Fiori app, it flows into journal entries automatically, and it is available as a first-class field for CDS views, custom reports, and statutory extractors.

Activation in Three Screens

The activation is a linear flow: open the configuration activity, pick the company codes that need it, and post an invoice to confirm the field is live.

Step 1 — Open Configuration Activity 107263

In your configuration environment, search for Activate Invoice Receipt Date (Configuration Activity 107263). The SSCUI is the single entry point for this setting.

Step 2 — Select the Company Codes

Add each company code that should expose the Invoice Receipt Date field. The activation is per company code, so you can pilot the feature in one entity before rolling it out group-wide.

Step 3 — Post a Supplier Invoice to Verify

Open Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) — or any of the FI/AP posting apps — and the new Invoice Receipt Date field appears alongside the Document Date and Posting Date. Once filled, the value is persisted in BKPF-REINDAT and is immediately available for reporting.

Practical Takeaway

To roll this out cleanly:

  • Identify the company codes that need the Invoice Receipt Date (start with entities subject to statutory payment-practice reporting).
  • Open Configuration Activity 107263 in the configuration environment.
  • Activate the setting for the selected company codes.
  • Test with one supplier invoice in F0859 — confirm the field appears and posts to BKPF-REINDAT.
  • Align your AP workflow so the mailroom / OCR / IDP tool populates the field automatically for every incoming invoice.

Summary

The Invoice Receipt Date has finally graduated from a niche public-sector field to a standard journal-entry attribute available to every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer. One SSCUI, one flag per company code, and your organization has a compliance-ready foundation for payment-practice reporting and receipt-based payment KPIs — without a custom field in sight.

Key Takeaways

  • Global availability: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 makes the Invoice Receipt Date (BKPF-REINDAT) available in all countries/regions, not just Public Sector or a few localizations.
  • Customer-controlled activation: Configuration Activity 107263 — Activate Invoice Receipt Date turns the field on per company code — no SAP Note, no code change.
  • Standard field, standard reporting: Because the value lives in BKPF-REINDAT, it flows into CDS views, extractors, and statutory reports as a first-class attribute — retiring the custom Z-fields many customers have relied on for years.

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal — Activate Invoice Receipt Date for All Countries/Regions (What's New 2608)
  • SAP Help Portal — Activating the Invoice Receipt Date (Country/Region-Specific Functions)
  • Scope item J60 — Accounts Payable
  • Application component FI-LOC-FI

Published by SAP Globalization Services · Feedback and questions welcome in the comments below.

entry.title: What's New in Review Booklets and MDA in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608
entry.content:
Data-driven decision-making depends on having the right insights at the right time, presented in a way that's easy to analyze and share. With the latest enhancements for Review Booklets and Multidimensional Analysis (MDA) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, users and designers gain powerful new capabilities to explore data, customize visualizations, streamline filtering, and create more flexible reporting experiences.

From new calculation options and number filters to advanced chart settings, enhanced app state handling, and major improvements in the Review Booklet Designer, these innovations help business users work more efficiently while enabling authors to build richer and more dynamic analytical content. Let's take a closer look at what's new.

What's New in Review Booklets and MDA

Calculations

In review booklet and MDA apps, you can apply calculation logic directly on measures using a new Calculations context menu and decide how the result or value of a measure is displayed. The context menu offers various options in two submenus that are called Display Values As and Display Totals As.

For more information, see Calculations and Using Calculations.

Number Filters

Review booklet and MDA apps now include a Number Filters panel and a context menu. You can use number filters to filter and rank numeric values based on your own rules.

To define number filters, open the Number Filters dialog using either the Number Filter option in the context menu or the Number Filters panel in the side panel. Select the context and measure, then set the condition: either numeric or ranking (for example, top or bottom N values). For more information, see Number Filters and Working with Number Filters.

 

Figure 1: Number Filters dialog

Chart Settings Side Panel

You can use a new Chart Settings panel in review booklet apps to configure charts the same way as in multidimensional analysis apps. The panel enables on-the-fly switching between table and chart views and provides advanced chart configuration options. The panel is available on pages with a single pivot table layout as well as a layout with two data providers (chart only).

You can freely toggle between table and chart view and use the following features:

  • Select the chart type
  • Show or hide the legend and change its position
  • Enable or disable axis labels
  • Adjust the value axis baseline, including manually setting minimum and maximum values

For more information, see Chart Settings Side Panel and Chart Settings.

Figure 2: Chart Settings side panel

Enhanced Export Features

The Export function in review booklet and MDA apps now includes Google Workspace integration, Google Sheets format support, and a streamlined Export to PDF option. The following features are available:

Google Workspace Integration

You can use Google Workspace as a destination. When you activate cloud enablement, the system sets Cloud destination as the default option. Otherwise, Local is the only available destination. For more information, see Google Workspace Integration.

Google Sheets Format Support

When you integrate Google Workspace with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can export data in Google Sheets format. For more information, see Google Workspace Integration.

Figure 3: Google Sheets format support

Streamlined Export to PDF Function

The styling of PDF reports has been refined to improve readability and visual consistency.

Export to Spreadsheet

The exported spreadsheet file contains two sheets: The data sheet contains all tabular data with comments if you select them in the configuration dialog. And the Information on Query sheet lists all query-relevant information, for example, Tenant URL and Query ID.

Export to PDF

The exported PDF report supports both single data provider and two data providers. You can use the PDF configuration dialog to choose which sections to export.

For more information, see  Enhanced Export Features and Export Data to Spredsheet and PDF.

Migrate Variants and Bookmarks App

Using this app, you can migrate multidimensional data grid (Web Dynpro Grid) app variants and bookmarks to the corresponding Multidimensional Analysis (MDA) apps.

If you've been using Web Dynpro Grid reporting applications and your organization is moving to Multidimensional Analysis (MDA) applications, you can keep your saved variants and bookmarks by the Migrate Variants and Bookmarks app.

Your existing variants (saved filter settings) and bookmarks (saved report views with specific data selections) from Web Dynpro Grid reports can be transferred to MDA apps. You can continue working with your personalized report settings without recreating them manually.

 

 


Figure 4: The Migrate Variants and Bookmarks app

For more information, see Migrate Variants and Bookmarks (What's New) and Migrate Variants and Bookmarks (app description).

Usability Improvements

Typeahead in the Filter Bar

While you're entering a filter criterion in one of the filter fields in review booklet and MDA apps, the system proposes available entries to you. Click one of the proposals to select it.

This helps you find the right filter values faster, reduces typing effort, and avoids errors by ensuring you select valid entries. For more information about booklet filters, see Typeahead in Filter Bar and Working with Booklet Filters.

Enhanced App State Handling

Review booklet apps offer enhanced app state handling: Whenever you modify the layout, adjust variables, apply filters, or switch pages, review booklets now generate a new app state that is visible in the URL.

If the application times out, you can simply refresh the browser to restore your session. This ensures that ongoing work is not lost and provides a seamless recovery experience. As a result, review booklets now behave consistently with standard Fiori applications when it comes to session handling and state restoration. Multidimensional Analysis applications already supported automatic state creation during user interactions.

Previously, review booklets did not automatically generate a new app state when you made changes. An updated state was only created when one of the three sharing options (Send E-Mail, Microsoft Teams, Save as Tile) was used.

For more information, see Enhanced App State Handling and Working with Views.

 

What's New in the Review Booklet Designer

Flexible Content Types for Review Booklets with Multiple Data Providers

When configuring the business pages of a custom review booklet with two data providers, you can choose different content types for each data provider. By selecting Choose Content Type, a dialog opens where separate display options can be defined for the two data providers. For example, users can now configure:

  • A table for one data provider and a chart for the other data provider
  • Two different tables for the two data providers
  • Two different chart types for the two data providers

For more information, see Flexible Content Types for Review Booklets with Multiple Data Providers and Business Pages.

WYSIWYG Editor Support for Pages with Two Data Providers

When you configure a custom review booklet with two data providers, you can use the WYSIWYG mode.

Previously, the WYSIWYG editor allowed users to create pages that contained one data provider only. This restricted users in their ability to design more complex or comparative layouts directly within the editor.

With the latest enhancement, the WYSIWYG editor now fully supports pages that include two data providers. When you create or edit such a page, a dialog is available where you can individually configure the left and right side of the layout. This includes selecting different layouts or visualization options for each side, offering much greater flexibility in designing review booklet content.

For more information on your layout options, see WYSIWYG Editor Support for Pages with Two Data Providers and Save Layout in WYSIWYG Mode.

 

Figure 5: Pages with two data providers in the WSYIWIG editor

The latest enhancements to Review Booklets, MDA applications, and the Review Booklet Designer further strengthen SAP's self-service analytics capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Whether you're analyzing key business metrics, creating executive-ready review booklets, or designing sophisticated multi-provider layouts, these new features offer greater flexibility, improved usability, and a more seamless user experience.

Explore the new capabilities in your system and visit the SAP Help Portal documentation to learn more about how these innovations can help you transform data into actionable insights:

Review Booklets

Multidimensional Analysis

Creating Review Booklets in the Review Booklet Designer

entry.title: SAP RAP : Dynamically manipulating fields based on Parent field value.
entry.content:
Step 1: Define Control Fields in CDS View

Create boolean control fields like isAppleBrand, isMaleGender in your CDS view.

define view ZMobileDevice as select from MobileDevice {
  key ID,
  Brand,
  ModelVariant,
  Gender,
  case Brand when 'Apple' then abap_true else abap_false end as isAppleBrand,
  case Gender when 'Male' then abap_true else abap_false end as isMaleGender
}

 

Step 2: Use Annotations for Conditional Visibility

Use @ui.Hidden annotation with control fields.

@ui.hidden: 'isAppleBrand'
ModelVariant

@UI.hidden: 'isMaleGender'
PregnancyStatus

@UI.hidden: 'isMaleGender'
MaternityLeave

Step 3: Enable Side Effects

Ensure side effects are triggered when the parent field changes. Handle this in Behaviour Definition.

determination setVisibility on modify { field Brand, Model;  }
  side effects { field Brand affects field isAppleBrand; }

 

Step 4: Behaviour Implementation 

METHOD setVisiblity.
    DATA: lt_update TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE <root view>.

    READ ENTITIES OF <root view> IN LOCAL MODE
        ENTITY <entity>
        FIELDS ( Brand Model )
        WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
        RESULT DATA(lt_mob_data).


    DATA lt_update_fields TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE <root view>.
    DATA ls_update_fields LIKE LINE OF lt_update_fields.

*
data(ls_entity) = lt_mob_data[ 1 ]-entity.


    lt_update = VALUE #( FOR ls_mob IN lt_mob_data (
          %tky = ls_mob-%tky

          " Set the boolean field based on ProjectType
          IsAppleBrand = xsdbool( ls_pir-Brande <> '01' OR ls_pir-Brand IS INITIAL )
         
          " Use the control structure to signal which fields should be updated
          %control = VALUE #(
                              IsAppleBrand = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                               )
    ) ) ).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF <rootview> IN LOCAL MODE
         ENTITY <entity>
         UPDATE FROM lt_update
         REPORTED DATA(lt_reported).

    reported = CORRESPONDING #( DEEP lt_reported ).


  ENDMETHOD.

Dynamic field control in SAP RAP enhances user experience by tailoring the UI to the context. Whether you're building a mobile configuration app or a gender-sensitive form, these techniques help you deliver clean, relevant interfaces.

entry.title: BKP - Flexible Plant Determination Through BadI for Customer Returns
entry.content:
Returns processing is one of the more complex areas of order management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. A question that comes up frequently when setting up returns is: how does the system decide which plant should receive the returned goods?In many real-world scenarios, such as large enterprises with complex organizational structures spanning multiple company codes and, as a result, many plants, the answer depends on a combination of business rules — rules that are too specific to handle through standard master data or configuration alone.

This blog explains how to use the Flexible Plant Determination feature to implement custom logic for plant determination in returns order items.

Flexible Plant Determination

Flexible plant determination lets you implement custom logic to determine the receiving plant for returns order items (document type CBAR), instead of relying solely on plant determination from master data, configuration, or reference documents.

The custom logic is implemented by using the Business Add-In (BAdI):

SD_SLS_DETERMINE_RETURNS_PLANT — Determination of Plants in Returns Order Items

This BAdI is called when a returns order is created or changed. It gives you access to a rich set of document attributes, such as sales area, sold-to party, return reason, business partner data, and even custom fields — which you can evaluate to determine the appropriate plant. This makes it possible to satisfy complex business requirements that cannot be expressed through standard configuration.

Plant Determination Priority

Before diving into the implementation, it is important to understand the overall plant determination sequence and when custom logic comes to play.

As a general rule, when creating or changing a returns order, the system determines the plant in the following order:

1Manual EntryIf you enter a plant directly in the item, this value takes precedence over all other determination logic.
2Custom Logic (by BAdI)

If no manual entry is provided, the system calls the BAdI implementation (if one exists). If the BAdI returns a plant, this value is used.

3Reference DocumentIf no custom logic applies and a reference document exists, the system copies the plant from the reference document.
4Standard DeterminationIf none of the above applies, the system determines the plant using master data and configuration.

However, depending on different channels (different apps or APIs) you use to process customer returns, there are exceptions to the priority order. For details, see the following graphic:

Prerequisites for Using BAdI Logic

Before you can use the BAdI, you must activate the flexible plant determination feature in the configuration activity:

Configure Returns Order Type for Standard Return (ID: 106396)

If this feature is not activated, the system will not call your BAdI implementation at all — custom logic will simply be ignored.

Note: Activating this feature also causes the system to redetermine the plant when ship-to party data or return reason changes on the document. This happens regardless of whether you have a BAdI implementation in place. But if you use the classic UI for returns order processing, such as the “Create Customer Returns Without Reference” app, the plant redetermination is automatically supported without activation.

Fields Available for Custom Logic

The BAdI provides access to header, item, and partner data of the returns order. The custom fields created for the document header and items are also available in BAdI implementation.

You can use these fields as input for your logic:

Returns Order Header DataReturns Order Item DataHeader Partner DataItem Partner Data
  • Returns order number
  • Sales organization
  • Distribution channel
  • Division
  • Sold-to party
  • Order reason
  • Reference document
  • Reference document category
  • Customer reference
  • <Any custom fields>
Note: Header data change does not trigger plant redetermination. See Important Notes at the end of the blog for details.
  • Returns order number
  • Returns order item number
  • Product
  • Return reason
  • Reference document
  • Reference document item
  • Reference document category
  • <Any custom fields>
  • Partner function
  • Customer
  • Supplier
  • Personnel
  • Contact person
  • Country
  • Region
  • Postal code
  • Address ID
  • Returns order item number
  • Partner function
  • Customer
  • Supplier
  • Personnel
  • Contact person
  • Country
  • Region
  • Postal code
  • Address ID

 For more information on custom fields, see help documents for respective apps or APIs extensibility.

Implementing Custom Plant Determination Logic

Approach 1: Direct Logic in the BAdI

For straightforward cases, you can implement the determination rules directly inside the BAdI method. To do so, use the Custom Logic app as an extensibility specialist (business role template: SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC). In the Custom Logic app, search for SD_SLS_DETERMINE_RETURNS_PLANT and create an implementation with your custom code.

Example:

IF     customerreturn-SDDocumentReason  = '009'
   AND customerreturn-SalesOrganization = '1010'.
    LOOP AT customerreturnitem_partners INTO item_partner
      WHERE SalesDocumentItem = customerreturnitem-SalesDocumentItem
        AND PartnerFunction   = 'WE'.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF item_partner-country = 'DE'
   AND item_partner-region  = 'BE'.
        plant = '1020'.
    RETURN.
    ENDIF.
ENDIF.

IF customerreturnitem-returnreason = '001'.
    plant = '1030'.
ENDIF.

In this example, the system determines the plant using this rule: If the order reason is 009 and the sales organization is 1010, the logic checks the ship-to party (partner function WE) on the item. If that partner is located in Germany (DE) and the Berlin region (BE), the system assigns plant 1020.

If the system can't find a plant using the first rule, then it continues to find using the second rule: If the return reason on the item is 001, the system assigns plant 1030.

This approach is easy to set up and works well when the number of rules is small and changes are infrequent. When the rule set grows — for example, when multiple sales organizations, regions, and return reasons each map to different plants — consider moving to a decision table as described in Approach 2.

Approach 2: Using a Decision Table

For more complex scenarios — where the determination depends on multiple conditions and combinations of field values — maintaining logic directly in code can quickly become difficult to read and maintain. Every new rule requires a code change, a transport, and a test cycle.

When to consider a decision table:

  • You have more than a handful of determination rules.
  • The rules change frequently and should be maintainable without developer involvement.
  • Multiple field combinations map to different plants and the combinations are expected to grow over time.
  • You want to document determination logic in a structured, auditable format.

In such cases, you can externalize the mapping using a decision table:

  1. Create a custom business object (for example, a Z-table) to store combinations of field values (such as sales organization, return reason, sold-to party region) alongside their corresponding plant values.
  2. At runtime, read this object from within the BAdI implementation and match the incoming document data against the stored rules to determine the plant.

This approach decouples the determination rules from the BAdI code. Business users or configurators can update the table without touching ABAP, which significantly reduces the cost of ongoing maintenance.

For more information, see Creating Custom Business Objects.

Step-by-Step Example: Using a Decision Table

Step 1: Open the Custom Business Objects app as extensibility specialist.

Step 2: Create a new business object with the required information and settings.

Check data is correct on the Nodes tab and change it if necessary:

Step 3: On the Fields tab, add the fields for your decision table and publish the business object.

Step 4: Once publishing completes successfully, use the UI link to access the decision table interface.

Step 5: In the decision table, maintain all the value mappings you need to determine plants.

Step 6: Implement custom code in the BAdI to read the business object data and determine the plant with matching data.

LOOP AT customerreturnitem_partners INTO item_partner
  WHERE SalesDocumentItem = customerreturnitem-SalesDocumentItem
    AND PartnerFunction   = 'WE'.
ENDLOOP.
SELECT Plant
  FROM YY1_PLANTDECISIONTABLE WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
  WHERE SalesOrganization = @customerreturn-salesorganization
    AND SDDocumentReason  = @customerreturn-SDDocumentReason
    AND Division          = @customerreturn-organizationdivision
    AND Country           = @item_partner-country
    AND PostalCode        = @item_partner-postalcode
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_plant)
  UP TO 1 ROWS.
IF lt_plant IS NOT INITIAL.
  plant = lt_plant[ 1 ]-plant.
ENDIF.

Important Notes

·      Header Data Changes Do Not Trigger Redetermination:

Once a plant has been determined by custom logic, subsequent changes to header data do not re-trigger plant determination for that item — even if the updated data would match a different rule. If you need the plant to be redetermined when header data changes, implement the additional BAdI:

SD_SLS_REPROCESS_ITEMS_HEAD — Reprocessing of Sales Document Items Based on Header Data Changes

·      Order Reason Behavior When Creating Reference via API:

When creating a returns order with reference using APIs, be aware of the following: if your custom logic uses the order reason as a condition, the system will not apply that logic to determine the plant. This is because the system first reads the order reason from the reference document, then overwrites it with the value from the payload. Since the change is purely a header data update, redetermination is not triggered and your custom logic is never called.

Workaround: Specify a return reason at the item level in your API payload and your BAdI logic will be applied.

Summary

Flexible plant determination gives you a clean, supported extension point for implementing business-specific returns routing logic in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The BAdI SD_SLS_DETERMINE_RETURNS_PLANT provides the flexibility to meet your business requirements. Here is a quick recap of the key points:

  • Activation: Must be enabled in Configure Order Type for Standard Return
  • Priority: Custom logic runs after manual entry but before reference document and standard determination
  • Simple rules: Implement directly in the BAdI method
  • Complex rules: Externalize mappings to a decision table (custom business object) for easier maintenance

For more information, see the SAP Help documentation: Flexible Plant Determination for Returns Order Items.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP 2608
entry.content:
Welcome to the second half of the year!

I’m thrilled to introduce SAP Cloud ERP 2608, the latest release designed to enhance productivity and innovation. This update offers new features that streamline operations, provide real-time analytics, and improve user experiences. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 equips businesses of all sizes with the tools needed to thrive in today's fast-paced environment. Discover how these enhancements can unlock new potential for growth and success.

I invite you to explore the latest updates by visiting the Release Navigator, your go-to resource for staying ahead with SAP Cloud ERP. As of today, content inside the Release Navigator will begin to be updated to 2608 through August! 

Guided Tours in the Release Navigator

Visit the "Get Assistance" section, where you can access guided tours to enhance your understanding and usage of the platform!

Explore the 'What's New' tour, which highlights the latest features and enhancements of the Release Navigator. This focused tour is designed to keep you up-to-date with the newest tools and functionalities available.

If you're visiting the Release Navigator for the first time, I highly recommend starting with the guided tour, "Using the Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP." The guided tour offers step-by-step instructions, useful tips, and insightful explanations to help you understand how to maximize the benefits of the Release Navigator. To start the guided tour, simply click on the green banner at the top of the page. Discover the full potential of the Release Navigator—it’s an excellent introduction to your exploration journey!
